commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an early return to `setDeep` for empty key arrays and removes the empty-string fallback from the final key assignment in Chrome profile decoration.

PR surface: Source +1. Total +1 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. at the private-helper source level: current main would write `obj[""]` if `setDeep` received an empty key array. There is no high-confidence user-path reproduction because all current production callers pass non-empty literal arrays.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated repair is appropriate; the remaining action is contributor real behavior proof or maintainer acceptance of the defensive cleanup.

target.title: fix: guard setDeep against empty keys array in Chrome profile decoration
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where `setDeep()` with an empty `keys` array would access `keys[-1]` (which is `undefined`), and the `?? ""` fallback would silently create a property with an empty string key (`""`) on the target object.

## Why This Change Was Made

Add an early `if (keys.length === 0) return;` guard at the top of `setDeep`, and remove the now-unnecessary `?? ""` fallback on the final key access. The production callers (`decorateOpenClawProfile`, `ensureProfileCleanExit`) pass non-empty literal arrays, so this is a defensive guard.

## User Impact

No user-visible change. Prevents silent pollution of Chrome profile objects in edge cases.

## Evidence

- **`extensions/browser/src/browser/profiles.test.ts`: 36/36 passed** ✅
- **`extensions/browser/src/browser/profiles-service.test.ts`: all tests pass**
- Production callers use hardcoded non-empty key arrays: `["profile","info_cache","Default","name"]`, etc.
